channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global […]

The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global justice and international institutional management.

The position is hosted within the Human Resources Section of the ICC Registry and provides selected candidates with hands-on experience in strategic HR management, organizational development, policy implementation, workforce planning, recruitment operations, learning and development, and international administration.

The application deadline is 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time).

About the International Criminal Court (ICC)

The International Criminal Court is one of the world’s leading international judicial institutions, responsible for investigating and prosecuting individuals accused of genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and crimes of aggression.

Headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands, the ICC operates with professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. The organization provides an international working environment focused on justice, accountability, professionalism, diversity, and global cooperation.

The Human Resources Section plays a critical role in supporting the Court’s global mission by ensuring effective workforce management, organizational efficiency, strategic talent development, and compliance with international administrative standards.

Overview of the JPO Position

Position Title

Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO)

Duty Station

The Hague, Netherlands

Organization

International Criminal Court (ICC)

Organizational Unit

Human Resources Section, Division of Management Services, Registry

Contract Duration

One-year appointment with possibility of extension according to the JPO Memorandum of Understanding

Annual Salary

Minimum Net Annual Salary (including post adjustment): €71,173

Application Deadline

26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time)

What Makes This Opportunity Significant?

This JPO role offers early-career professionals the rare opportunity to:

  • Work directly within a major international organization
  • Gain exposure to UN Common System HR frameworks
  • Participate in strategic HR and organizational development projects
  • Build expertise in international recruitment and policy implementation
  • Contribute to organizational transformation initiatives
  • Develop experience in multicultural and multinational workplace management
  • Work alongside global experts in administration, HR operations, and institutional governance

For candidates interested in long-term careers with the United Nations system, international NGOs, global institutions, or diplomatic organizations, this opportunity provides outstanding career-building exposure.

Human Resources Section at the ICC

The Human Resources Section provides centralized HR advisory, administrative, and strategic support across the Court. It operates within the Division of Management Services alongside:

  • Budget Section
  • Finance Section
  • Safety and Security Section
  • General Services Section
  • Occupational Health Unit
  • SAP Competence Centre

The HR Section focuses on both operational and strategic human resource management functions.

Key Areas Managed by the HR Section

The section oversees:

  • Recruitment and onboarding
  • Workforce planning
  • Job design and classification
  • Payroll administration
  • Employee benefits and entitlements
  • Pension and insurance management
  • Performance management
  • Organizational development
  • Learning and development programmes
  • Career planning and succession management
  • Staff relations and grievance support

Structure of the Human Resources Section

The HR Section consists of two specialized units:

1. HR Operations Unit

This unit handles operational HR functions including:

  • Recruitment processes
  • Staff onboarding
  • Compensation administration
  • Payroll management
  • Benefits and entitlements
  • Post management
  • Health insurance administration
  • Pension administration
  • HR-related staff complaints and grievances
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) staff data management

2. HR Organizational Development Unit

This unit focuses on strategic HR initiatives such as:

  • Workforce planning
  • Talent management
  • Organizational design
  • Staff development
  • Training programmes
  • Learning and development strategies
  • Performance management systems
  • Leadership and managerial development

Duties and Responsibilities

Under the supervision of the Head of the Human Resources Operations Unit, the selected JPO will support multiple HR functions and strategic projects.

Main Responsibilities Include:

  1. Supporting implementation of HR-related projects and strategic initiatives
  2. Conducting research on HR policies, procedures, and best practices from comparable international organizations
  3. Assisting in the development of HR guidelines and institutional procedures
  4. Liaising with HR specialists on recruitment, entitlements, insurance, policy interpretation, and administrative matters
  5. Reviewing HR issues and presenting analytical recommendations to management
  6. Supporting the monitoring and evaluation of HR policies and organizational practices
  7. Assisting in the development of HR performance indicators and management tools
  8. Contributing to Service Level Agreements and HR work plan tracking systems
  9. Conducting preliminary policy analysis and assisting in drafting position papers
  10. Designing internal communication materials supporting HR objectives and institutional goals
  11. Providing support across HR units whenever required
  12. Performing additional duties assigned by HR leadership

Required Qualifications

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess either:

  • An advanced university degree in:
    • Human Resources Management
    • Business Administration
    • Public Administration
    • Law
    • Social Sciences
    • Information Management
    • Or another related field

OR

  • A first-level university degree combined with two additional years of qualifying experience

Professional Experience Requirements

Candidates must have:

  • Minimum two years of relevant HR experience

OR

  • Four years of experience if applying with a first-level university degree

Desirable Experience

Additional advantages include experience in:

  • International organizations
  • UN Common System salary and allowance structures
  • Broad HR operations and administration
  • HR policy implementation
  • Organizational development initiatives

Required Skills and Competencies

The ICC seeks candidates with strong technical and interpersonal competencies.

Important Skills Include:

  • Human resources management knowledge
  • Strong analytical and research skills
  • Strategic planning capabilities
  • Results-oriented work approach
  • Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Professional writing ability
  • Cross-cultural collaboration skills
  • Confidentiality and discretion
  • Flexibility and motivation
  • Strong professional integrity

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English or French

Knowledge of the second working language is considered desirable.

Additional knowledge of official ICC languages such as:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • Russian
  • Spanish

will be considered an advantage.

Eligibility Requirements for Republic of Korea JPO Applicants

This position is specifically sponsored by the Republic of Korea and therefore includes additional eligibility conditions.

Applicants must:

  • Be Korean citizens
  • Be fluent in Korean and English
  • Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree
  • Meet English proficiency requirements
  • Be under 32 years old as of 31 December 2026
  • Have completed or be exempt from Korean military service (for male applicants)

Accepted English Test Scores

Applicants must provide valid English test results meeting at least one of the following minimum scores:

  • TOEFL iBT: 100
  • TEPS: 430
  • IELTS: 7
  • TOEIC: 900

Additional notes:

  • TOEFL Home Edition is accepted
  • TOEFL MyBest Scores are not accepted
  • IELTS Academic and General Training are both accepted
  • Scores remain valid for two years after the test date

Military Service Age Extension

The maximum age limit may be extended for applicants who completed Korean military service:

  • One-year extension for service under one year
  • Two-year extension for service between one and two years
  • Three-year extension for service of two years or more

Required Documents

Applicants must submit all documents in English through the ICC e-recruitment system.

Required Documents Include:

  1. Resident Register issued by the Korean Government
  2. University degree certificates
  3. English proficiency certificate
  4. Employment certificates detailing:
    • Employment period
    • Organization
    • Department
    • Position title
    • Duties and responsibilities
  5. Military service certificate (if applicable)
  6. National Basic Livelihood Security certificate (for low-income applicants)
  7. Disability certificate (for applicants with disabilities)

Why You Should Apply

This ICC JPO opportunity offers substantial professional and international career benefits, including:

  • International HR experience
  • Exposure to global institutional operations
  • High-level policy and organizational development work
  • Competitive international salary
  • Multicultural professional environment
  • Networking with global professionals
  • Career pathway into international organizations and the UN system
  • Professional growth in strategic HR management

Final Thoughts

The ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO Programme 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious Korean professionals seeking international exposure in human resources management and organizational development.

Working at the International Criminal Court provides not only a competitive international career pathway but also the opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s most significant global justice institutions.

Candidates with strong HR expertise, analytical capabilities, international outlook, and commitment to public service are strongly encouraged to apply before the 26 June 2026 deadline.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the ICC Associate Human Resources Officer job opportunity.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding & Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards. This global initiative is designed […]

The post Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding & Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards.

This global initiative is designed to empower social impact organizations that are creating meaningful change in communities around the world. Through this grant, selected organizations will receive up to $10,000 USD worth of professional design, marketing, branding, and AI-powered business support services to help amplify their mission and increase visibility.

The application deadline is August 19, 2026, in recognition of World Humanitarian Day.

About the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant

The “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant is more than a funding opportunity. It is a visibility and growth initiative created specifically for organizations that are driving social transformation but may lack the branding, communication, or digital infrastructure needed to expand their reach and impact.

Bombshell Impact Design Agency believes that many world-changing missions remain unseen not because of lack of value, but because of limited visibility, storytelling, and strategic positioning. This initiative aims to bridge that gap by helping impactful organizations strengthen how they communicate and present their mission to the world.

The program welcomes applications from organizations across every region globally, reinforcing the agency’s commitment to supporting changemakers regardless of geographic location.

Grant Benefits

Selected recipients can receive up to $10,000 USD in professional services, including:

  • Branding and identity development
  • Strategic marketing support
  • AI-powered creative and communication services
  • Digital visibility enhancement
  • Storytelling and campaign support
  • Design solutions tailored to mission-driven organizations
  • Strategic impact communication support

These services are intended to help organizations improve outreach, attract supporters, strengthen campaigns, and scale their impact more effectively.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many social impact organizations struggle with limited resources for branding, communication, and digital marketing. While their missions may be transformative, they often face challenges in:

  • Building public awareness
  • Reaching donors and supporters
  • Creating compelling digital campaigns
  • Developing strong visual identities
  • Competing for visibility online
  • Communicating measurable impact effectively

This grant directly addresses those challenges by providing expert-level creative and strategic services that many organizations would otherwise struggle to afford.

Who Can Apply?

The opportunity is open to social impact organizations worldwide that are actively working to create positive change in areas such as:

  • Sustainability
  • Environmental protection
  • Community development
  • Humanitarian support
  • Education
  • Accessibility
  • Social justice
  • Health and wellbeing
  • Youth empowerment
  • Innovation for social good

Organizations of different sizes and stages are encouraged to apply, especially those with compelling missions and measurable impact stories.

How to Apply

Applicants must complete a simple multi-step application process.

Step 1: Complete the Application Form

Organizations are required to provide information about:

  • Their mission
  • Their organization
  • The impact they are creating
  • Their long-term goals
  • The communities they serve

Applicants should clearly explain how the grant support would help expand or strengthen their work.

Step 2: Upload a Short Video

Applicants must submit a 30–60 second video filmed on a phone or simple camera setup.

The video should:

  • Introduce the organization
  • Share the mission and impact story
  • Explain why the work matters
  • Highlight the organization’s vision for change

The agency specifically encourages applicants to share their story authentically and in their own voice.

Step 3: Application Review and Winner Selection

After the deadline closes, all applications will be reviewed carefully by the Bombshell Impact Design Agency team. Selected winners will then be announced following the evaluation process.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: August 19, 2026

The deadline coincides with World Humanitarian Day, emphasizing the global importance of humanitarian and social impact initiatives.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare a compelling story-driven application.

A Global Opportunity for Changemakers

One of the strongest aspects of this initiative is its international accessibility. Organizations from every continent are eligible to apply, making this a truly global social impact opportunity.

Whether an organization is based in Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania, or the Middle East, they are encouraged to submit their application if they are making meaningful contributions toward positive change.

The Core Message Behind the Initiative

The campaign carries a powerful message:

“The sky was never the limit. Visibility was.”

This reflects the idea that many organizations already possess the passion, innovation, and commitment needed to change the world. What they often need most is strategic visibility and stronger communication support to reach larger audiences and unlock greater opportunities.

Why Organizations Should Apply

This grant presents a unique opportunity for social impact organizations to gain access to high-value professional services without direct financial cost.

Organizations should consider applying because it offers:

  • International visibility
  • Access to expert branding support
  • Professional marketing services
  • AI-driven creative solutions
  • Increased awareness opportunities
  • Stronger communication tools
  • Enhanced storytelling capacity
  • Strategic mission amplification

For organizations seeking growth, awareness, and stronger public engagement, this initiative could become a major catalyst for expansion and long-term impact.

Final Thoughts

The Bombshell Impact Design Agency Social Impact Grant 2026 stands out as an innovative and mission-driven opportunity for organizations working toward meaningful global change.

Rather than focusing solely on financial funding, the initiative addresses one of the biggest modern challenges facing nonprofits and social enterprises: visibility.

For organizations with transformative missions but limited branding or marketing capacity, this grant could provide the professional support needed to elevate their message, strengthen their outreach, and expand their influence globally.

Interested organizations should begin preparing their applications, impact narratives, and short video submissions before the August 19, 2026 deadline.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Students interested in software engineering, technology, and innovation now have an exciting opportunity to gain firsthand exposure to one of the world’s leading financial institutions. The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 is officially accepting applications from students who are eager to explore careers in engineering, strengthen their technical abilities, and build meaningful professional networks. […]

The post Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students interested in software engineering, technology, and innovation now have an exciting opportunity to gain firsthand exposure to one of the world’s leading financial institutions. The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 is officially accepting applications from students who are eager to explore careers in engineering, strengthen their technical abilities, and build meaningful professional networks.

Designed specifically for students in their penultimate or final year of study, the Engineering Academy is a highly interactive two-day insight program that introduces participants to the world of software engineering at Goldman Sachs.

About the Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy

The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy is an immersive learning experience aimed at helping students better understand the role of engineering within the organization and the broader technology industry.

The program is open to students from all academic disciplines who have an interest in programming, technology, and using logical thinking to build scalable software solutions and systems.

Through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and collaborative activities, participants will gain valuable exposure to engineering careers while developing skills that can support future applications for software engineering opportunities.

Why Engineering Matters at Goldman Sachs

Engineering sits at the heart of Goldman Sachs’ global operations.

The firm’s engineers develop innovative technologies that power businesses, financial markets, and client solutions around the world. They combine cutting-edge technologies, open-source software, cloud computing solutions, databases, and programming languages to create applications that are accurate, scalable, and impactful.

Software engineers at Goldman Sachs work on complex challenges that require creativity, technical expertise, and collaboration. The Engineering Academy offers participants a chance to learn how these professionals contribute to solving real-world business problems through technology.

What Participants Will Gain

Students selected for the program will have the opportunity to:

1. Explore Engineering Career Opportunities

Participants will discover the wide range of career paths available within software engineering and technology at Goldman Sachs.

2. Develop Technical Skills

The program includes practical workshops and collaborative activities designed to strengthen technical knowledge and problem-solving abilities.

3. Build Soft Skills

In addition to technical development, students will improve essential professional skills such as:

  • Communication
  • Teamwork
  • Leadership
  • Collaboration
  • Critical thinking
  • Professional networking

4. Learn from Industry Professionals

Participants will interact directly with Goldman Sachs professionals and gain insights into their career journeys, experiences, and diverse backgrounds.

5. Expand Professional Networks

Students will connect with peers who share similar interests while building relationships with professionals working across engineering and technology teams.

6. Experience Goldman Sachs Culture

The program provides valuable insight into Goldman Sachs’ long-standing apprenticeship culture and commitment to professional growth and excellence.

Program Location

The 2026 Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy will take place in:

Birmingham, United Kingdom

The program will run over two days in September 2026.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the Engineering Academy, applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program.
  • Be graduating in 2027 or June 2028.
  • Have an interest in software engineering, programming, technology, and problem-solving.
  • Be interested in learning how engineering supports innovation within financial services and technology.

Importantly, the program welcomes students from all academic backgrounds, making it accessible to individuals who may not be pursuing traditional computer science or engineering degrees.

What Makes This Opportunity Valuable?

The Engineering Academy offers more than just a short learning experience. It serves as a platform for students to:

  • Explore future software engineering careers.
  • Gain exposure to a global financial institution.
  • Strengthen both technical and professional competencies.
  • Learn directly from experienced engineers.
  • Build a network of peers and industry professionals.
  • Better understand the skills needed to succeed in technology-focused careers.

For students considering a future in software engineering or technology, the program provides a valuable opportunity to gain practical insights before graduation.

Application Timeline

Application Deadline

21 June 2026

Notification Period

Applicants can expect to hear back from Goldman Sachs by July 2026.

Programme Dates

The Engineering Academy will take place over two days in September 2026.

How to Apply

Interested students should submit their applications through Goldman Sachs Careers.

Students are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the deadline.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for students to gain exposure to software engineering careers, strengthen their technical and professional skills, and connect with industry experts.

Whether you come from a technical or non-technical academic background, this programme offers a valuable introduction to the engineering teams that drive innovation across one of the world’s most influential organizations.

If you are graduating in 2027 or June 2028 and have an interest in technology, software development, and problem-solving, this programme could be an important step toward your future career.

For more information on this opportunity visit here 

Discover more opportunities on OFY, here 

The post Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The South African Research Chair in Violent Histories and Transgenerational Trauma (SARChI Chair), based at the Centre for the Study of the Afterlife of Violence and the Reparative Quest (AVReQ) at Stellenbosch University, has opened applications for its 2027 MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships. Funded by South Africa’s National Research Foundation (NRF), these […]

The post Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The South African Research Chair in Violent Histories and Transgenerational Trauma (SARChI Chair), based at the Centre for the Study of the Afterlife of Violence and the Reparative Quest (AVReQ) at Stellenbosch University, has opened applications for its 2027 MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships.

Funded by South Africa’s National Research Foundation (NRF), these prestigious scholarships support academically outstanding students who wish to pursue advanced research into the enduring legacies of violence, trauma, memory, healing, and social repair.

The programme provides a unique opportunity for emerging scholars to engage with critical questions surrounding violent histories, transgenerational trauma, psychosocial healing, and decolonial knowledge production within a collaborative and interdisciplinary academic environment.

About the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair

The MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair is a specialized postgraduate programme designed to equip scholars with the theoretical, methodological, and practical tools necessary to examine the complex and long-lasting consequences of violence across generations.

The programme focuses on:

  • Decolonial approaches to knowledge production
  • Violent histories and their continuing social impacts
  • Transgenerational trauma and memory
  • Psychosocial processes of healing and repair
  • Social justice and restorative practices
  • Community-based and collaborative research
  • Public engagement and intellectual contribution

Students will become part of the vibrant intellectual community at AVReQ, engaging with scholars, researchers, and practitioners working on some of the most pressing questions related to violence, justice, memory, and healing.

Scholarship Benefits

The scholarship supports full-time enrolment in the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair programme at Stellenbosch University.

Scholarship Duration

  • Two years of funded study

Programme Start Date

  • January 2027

Funding Structure

  • South African citizens and permanent residents may receive full NRF scholarships.
  • International students who are selected may receive partial scholarship funding.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

Applicants must:

  1. Hold an Honours degree or equivalent qualification before the MPhil programme begins.
  2. Have achieved a minimum final mark of 65% in their Honours degree.
  3. Have completed their Honours degree no earlier than 2024.
  4. Be able to provide proof of graduation or confirmation from a supervisor indicating expected graduation by the end of the 2026 academic year.

Research and Personal Commitment

Applicants must also demonstrate:

  • A strong commitment to postgraduate education and research.
  • A genuine interest in exploring issues related to violent histories, trauma, memory, justice, healing, and repair.
  • A willingness to contribute actively to a collaborative research community.
  • Intellectual curiosity and engagement with decolonial scholarship.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must first apply for admission to the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair through Stellenbosch University’s online application system.

In addition, candidates must submit a separate scholarship application by email.

The scholarship application package must include:

1. Academic Qualification Documents

  • Copy of Honours degree certificate, OR
  • Letter from a supervisor confirming expected graduation by the end of 2026

2. Sample of Academic Work

Applicants should provide one example of previous academic writing, such as:

  • Academic essay
  • Journal publication
  • Dissertation chapter
  • Honours research project chapter

3. Scholarship Motivation Letter (Maximum Three Pages)

The motivation letter should address:

  • Academic background and achievements
  • Leadership experience and community engagement
  • Professional, civic, or academic involvement
  • Research interests and alignment with the programme
  • Intended contribution to AVReQ’s intellectual and public life
  • Financial need (where applicable)

4. Recommendation Letter

Applicants must submit:

  • At least one recommendation letter from an academic mentor familiar with their work and capabilities

5. Declaration Form

Candidates must complete and submit:

  • Declaration of Authorship, Generative AI Use, and Writing Assistance Form

Submission Process

All scholarship materials should be submitted via email to:

AVREQINFO@sun.ac.za

Use the following email subject line:

MPHIL SCHOLARSHIP APPLICATION 2027

Application Deadline

All applications must be received by:

Monday, 22 June 2026 at 12:00 PM (Noon)

Late applications are unlikely to be considered.

Interview Process

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in interviews.

Interview Date

  • 25 June 2026

Applicants should ensure they are available on this date if shortlisted.

Why Apply?

This scholarship offers a rare opportunity to study one of the most urgent and relevant areas of contemporary scholarship.

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • NRF-funded postgraduate support
  • Access to leading scholars and researchers
  • Participation in a dynamic interdisciplinary research community
  • Training in decolonial and reparative scholarship
  • Opportunities to contribute to public discourse and social transformation
  • Engagement with critical questions surrounding trauma, healing, justice, and memory

For students committed to understanding and addressing the enduring effects of violence and historical injustice, the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair provides an exceptional platform for academic growth, research excellence, and meaningful societal impact.

For more information on this scholarship visit here 

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here 

The post Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Dalberg Research Fellow 2026 is a prestigious 12-month fellowship offered by Dalberg Advisors, a globally recognized impact consulting firm working at the intersection of strategy, evidence, design, and systems change. This opportunity is designed for experienced researchers, policy professionals, and development practitioners who want to shape the future of global development through high-impact research, […]

The post Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Dalberg Research Fellow 2026 is a prestigious 12-month fellowship offered by Dalberg Advisors, a globally recognized impact consulting firm working at the intersection of strategy, evidence, design, and systems change. This opportunity is designed for experienced researchers, policy professionals, and development practitioners who want to shape the future of global development through high-impact research, thought leadership, and innovation.

Based primarily in India, with remote and hybrid working options available, this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to contribute to a newly emerging research and thought leadership initiative within Dalberg focused on shaping global development agendas.

The fellowship is part of Dalberg’s effort to establish a forward-looking research engine that generates insights on major global challenges such as climate finance, gender equity, education systems, AI in development, and inclusive economic growth.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, with a preferred start date from June 2026 onward.

About Dalberg and Its Global Mission

Dalberg Advisors is part of the broader Dalberg Group, a global organization committed to building a more inclusive and sustainable world. With 28 offices worldwide and experience in over 135 countries, Dalberg integrates:

  • Strategic advisory expertise
  • Rigorous research and data analysis
  • Deep local and regional knowledge
  • Cross-sector collaboration with governments, NGOs, and private sector actors

Dalberg operates at the intersection of:

  • Strategy and policy design
  • Evidence-based development research
  • Systems transformation and innovation
  • Global impact consulting

The organization works across major global development themes including education, gender equity, climate change, digital infrastructure, and economic justice.

About the Research & Thought Leadership Initiative

The Research Fellow will contribute to a newly established Dalberg Observatory-style initiative, designed to produce forward-thinking research that shapes global development discourse.

This initiative focuses on generating research and insights in emerging global areas such as:

  • Future of outcomes-based funding and philanthropic capital flows
  • Systems change measurement and collective impact evaluation
  • Gender equity challenges, including boys’ education and underexplored inequalities
  • Artificial intelligence in education, work, and public services
  • Future of development financing and global policy innovation

The initiative aims to produce high-quality research publications, convenings, and thought leadership that influence policymakers, donors, and global institutions.

Key Objectives of the Research Fellow Role

The Research Fellow will play a central role in shaping and executing Dalberg’s research agenda by:

  • Leading or supporting original research on global development themes
  • Contributing to quarterly publications and research outputs
  • Translating internal consulting insights into global thought leadership
  • Developing policy-relevant research products and analytical frameworks
  • Supporting experimental research design and foresight analysis

Key thematic focus areas include:

  • Global development funding flows (climate, education, gender)
  • Systems change metrics and impact measurement frameworks
  • Education equity, especially boys’ education
  • AI applications in governance, public services, and labor markets

Key Responsibilities

The Research Fellow will engage in a wide range of analytical and strategic tasks, including:

1. Research and Analysis

  • Conduct structured desk research and literature reviews
  • Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis
  • Build comparative case studies across regions and sectors
  • Support data synthesis and interpretation

2. Writing and Publication

  • Draft research briefs, memos, blogs, and analytical papers
  • Develop thought leadership content for internal and external audiences
  • Contribute to quarterly publications and research outputs

3. Data Visualization and Insight Generation

  • Support development of data visualization products
  • Translate complex datasets into accessible insights
  • Assist in producing research presentations and reports

4. Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration

  • Engage with internal Dalberg teams globally
  • Support research partnerships and roundtable discussions
  • Assist in designing events and convenings on key topics

5. Institutional Development

  • Contribute to shaping the research initiative’s structure
  • Support operational design and strategic development
  • Help define long-term research direction and outputs

Desired Qualifications and Experience

Candidates are expected to bring a strong mix of analytical ability, research experience, and intellectual curiosity.

Required Qualifications

  • 4–8 years of experience in global development, research, or public policy
  • Master’s degree preferred in fields such as:
    • Public Policy
    • International Affairs
    • Sociology
    • Public Health
    • Data Journalism or related disciplines

Technical Skills

  • Strong writing, synthesis, and communication skills
  • Ability to translate complex ideas into accessible insights
  • Experience with at least one statistical tool:
    • Python
    • R
    • Stata
    • SPSS
  • Optional but valuable:
    • GIS mapping tools
    • Qualitative analysis software (NVivo, ATLAS.ti, MAXQDA)

Professional Experience

  • Experience in think tanks, research institutes, or policy organizations
  • Familiarity with development finance or global policy systems
  • Exposure to consulting or advisory environments is an advantage

Personal Attributes

  • Strong intellectual curiosity and independent thinking
  • Ability to work in ambiguous and evolving research environments
  • Capacity to produce rapid, high-quality research outputs
  • Self-motivated and impact-driven mindset

What This Fellowship Offers

The Dalberg Research Fellow role is designed as both a professional development opportunity and a potential long-term career pathway.

Participants will benefit from:

  • Opportunity to publish high-impact global development research
  • Mentorship from senior global experts
  • Exposure to international policy ecosystems
  • Access to high-level development networks
  • Participation in shaping a new research institution within Dalberg
  • Possibility of transitioning into a long-term leadership role
  • High-trust environment focused on innovation and intellectual freedom

This fellowship is ideal for professionals seeking to move into high-level research leadership, policy advisory roles, or global development strategy positions.

Application Timeline

  • Applications: Rolling basis
  • Preferred Start Date: From June 2026
  • Duration: 12 months (full-time fellowship)
  • Location: India (Remote/Hybrid options available)

Early application is strongly encouraged due to limited placement capacity and continuous review.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply directly through Dalberg Advisors’ official careers or recruitment channels, following instructions for the Research Fellow position.

Apply Here

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Dalberg Research Fellow role is more than a fellowship—it is a launchpad into the future of global development thinking. It offers participants the chance to influence how governments, donors, and global institutions approach complex challenges such as inequality, climate change, education reform, and digital transformation.

For researchers ready to move beyond traditional analysis and contribute to systems-level change, this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to shape global narratives and policy innovation at scale.

Discover more global fellowship positions on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Young scientists and innovators around the world now have an exceptional opportunity to gain international recognition for their contributions to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). UNESCO has officially launched the call for nominations and applications for the third edition of the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM. This […]

The post UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young scientists and innovators around the world now have an exceptional opportunity to gain international recognition for their contributions to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). UNESCO has officially launched the call for nominations and applications for the third edition of the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM.

This globally recognized biennial prize celebrates outstanding young researchers, innovators, educators, and scientific leaders whose work contributes to advancing scientific knowledge, strengthening STEM education, promoting innovation, and fostering international cooperation in support of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Five outstanding laureates from different regions of the world will each receive a monetary award of USD 50,000, in addition to a medal and diploma recognizing their achievements and contributions to society.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1)

Applicants and nominators are strongly encouraged to begin the application process early to allow sufficient time for account verification, nomination submission, and document preparation.

About the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM was established to strengthen scientific research, improve STEM education, encourage innovation, and enhance international scientific cooperation.

The Prize supports UNESCO’s broader mission of promoting science as a driver of sustainable development and recognizes young scientists whose work addresses some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including climate change, sustainable development, technological innovation, education, public health, environmental sustainability, and socio-economic advancement.

Through this initiative, UNESCO seeks to inspire the next generation of scientists while encouraging governments, institutions, and communities to invest in scientific excellence and innovation.

The Prize also places a strong emphasis on gender equality by encouraging the participation and recognition of women and girls in STEM disciplines.

Prize Benefits

Selected laureates will receive:

Financial Award

  • USD 50,000 for each laureate

International Recognition

  • UNESCO international recognition
  • Global visibility among scientific communities
  • Increased opportunities for collaboration and networking
  • Enhanced professional credibility

Official Honors

  • UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Medal
  • UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Diploma

Career Advancement Opportunities

  • Increased exposure to global scientific networks
  • Opportunities for international partnerships
  • Recognition from leading scientific institutions worldwide

Number of Awards Available

The Prize is awarded to five laureates every two years, representing the following geographical regions:

  1. Africa
  2. Arab States
  3. Asia and the Pacific
  4. Europe and North America
  5. Latin America and the Caribbean

This regional representation ensures global inclusion and recognizes scientific excellence across diverse communities and research environments.

Objectives of the Prize

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize aims to:

  • Promote excellence in STEM research
  • Encourage innovation and scientific discovery
  • Support STEM education and knowledge dissemination
  • Strengthen international scientific cooperation
  • Advance progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals
  • Increase participation of women and girls in STEM
  • Inspire future generations of scientists and innovators

Who Can Apply?

The Prize is open to young scientists, researchers, innovators, educators, and STEM professionals who have demonstrated exceptional contributions in their respective fields.

Candidates must have achieved significant recognition for their work and demonstrate measurable impact within their area of expertise.

Eligibility Criteria

Age Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be under 40 years of age on the application deadline date (30 November 2026)

Achievement Requirement

Candidates must have demonstrated significant achievements or contributions in one or more of the following areas:

1. STEM Research

Including:

  • Scientific research
  • Applied research
  • Emerging technologies
  • Breakthrough discoveries
  • Innovative scientific solutions

2. STEM Education and Innovation

Including:

  • STEM teaching and learning initiatives
  • Science communication
  • Dissemination of research findings
  • Educational innovation
  • Creativity and technological innovation

3. International or Regional Cooperation

Including:

  • Cross-border scientific collaboration
  • Regional research partnerships
  • International STEM initiatives
  • Scientific diplomacy
  • Knowledge-sharing programmes

Focus on Gender Equality

UNESCO strongly encourages nominations of young women scientists.

The Prize seeks to address gender disparities in STEM by recognizing female scientific leaders and inspiring more girls and young women to pursue careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

Application Process

Applicants must complete an online application dossier.

Step 1: Create an Online Account

Candidates must:

  • Register online
  • Verify their email address
  • Complete their profile information

Step 2: Complete the Application Dossier

Applicants must provide detailed information about:

  • Educational background
  • Professional achievements
  • Scientific contributions
  • Research impact
  • Innovation activities
  • International collaboration experience

Step 3: Upload Supporting Documents

Required documents include:

  • Description of background and achievements
  • Summary of research work and publications
  • Evidence of significant contributions
  • Statement explaining contribution to the Prize objectives
  • Additional supporting documentation

Step 4: Secure a Valid Nomination

Applications are only valid if accompanied by an eligible nomination.

Candidates must ensure their nominator submits the nomination before the application deadline.

Step 5: Submit Before the Deadline

Both the application and nomination must be completed by:

30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1)

Nomination Requirements

A nomination is mandatory.

Applications submitted without an eligible nomination will not be considered.

Who Can Nominate?

Eligible nominators include:

Government Institutions

  • Governments of UNESCO Member States
  • National Commissions for UNESCO

UNESCO Partners

  • UNESCO Chairs
  • Category 2 Institutes and Centres under UNESCO

Scientific Organizations

  • International scientific unions
  • Relevant scientific bodies

Non-Governmental Organizations

  • NGOs maintaining official partnerships with UNESCO
  • Organizations active in STEM-related fields

Universities

Eligible university representatives include:

  • Department Heads
  • Deans
  • University management officials

Individual faculty members and staff members are not eligible to submit nominations independently.

What Must a Nomination Include?

A nomination should contain:

Candidate Background

  • Professional profile
  • Educational background
  • Major achievements

Scientific Contributions

  • Publications
  • Research outputs
  • Innovation achievements
  • Major accomplishments

Contribution to Prize Objectives

  • Impact on STEM advancement
  • Educational contributions
  • Innovation and scientific leadership
  • International cooperation efforts

Important Application Notes

Applicants should carefully note the following:

  • Self-nominations alone are not accepted
  • Applications must be completed in English or French
  • Nominations must be completed in English or French
  • Incomplete submissions will be rejected
  • Late applications will not be accepted
  • Account verification may require additional time
  • Each nominating institution may nominate up to three candidates
  • Supporting documents must be submitted before the deadline

Why This Prize Matters

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize is one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions for emerging scientific leaders.

Beyond the financial award, the Prize provides recipients with international visibility, global recognition, and access to influential scientific networks that can accelerate their careers and amplify the impact of their work.

For young researchers and innovators dedicated to solving global challenges through science and technology, this Prize represents a powerful platform for showcasing excellence and contributing to sustainable development worldwide.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Interested candidates should begin preparing their applications and securing eligible nominators as early as possible to ensure successful submission before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM offers an extraordinary opportunity for young scientists, researchers, innovators, and educators to gain international recognition for their contributions to science and society.

With five awards of USD 50,000 each, global visibility, and recognition from UNESCO, the Prize celebrates the next generation of scientific leaders working to advance knowledge, innovation, education, and sustainable development around the world.

Eligible candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications and nominations well ahead of the 30 November 2026 deadline.

Click here for more global award opportunities

 

The post UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) has announced multiple international career opportunities for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, climate action, economic transformation, regional cooperation, and public administration across the Asia-Pacific region. These positions span senior leadership roles, programme management opportunities, and specialized consultancy assignments. Successful […]

The post United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) has announced multiple international career opportunities for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, climate action, economic transformation, regional cooperation, and public administration across the Asia-Pacific region.

These positions span senior leadership roles, programme management opportunities, and specialized consultancy assignments. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to work with one of the most influential regional bodies of the United Nations while helping member states achieve sustainable development goals and long-term economic resilience.

Applications are currently open for four positions, with deadlines ranging from 8 June to 4 July 2026.

About ESCAP

The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) serves as the regional development arm of the United Nations Secretariat.

ESCAP works to:

  • Promote sustainable and inclusive economic growth
  • Strengthen regional connectivity
  • Support climate action initiatives
  • Enhance policy development and implementation
  • Facilitate technical cooperation among member states
  • Advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Through policy research, technical assistance, capacity-building programmes, and regional cooperation initiatives, ESCAP supports countries in addressing emerging economic, environmental, and social challenges.

1. Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1)

Location

Almaty, Kazakhstan

Application Deadline

27 June 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is seeking an experienced international development leader to head its Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (SONCA).

The selected candidate will oversee ESCAP’s engagement with:

  • Armenia
  • Azerbaijan
  • Georgia
  • Kazakhstan
  • Kyrgyzstan
  • Russian Federation
  • Tajikistan
  • Turkmenistan
  • Uzbekistan

As the organization’s senior representative in the subregion, the successful candidate will lead policy dialogue, regional cooperation initiatives, stakeholder engagement, and strategic programme implementation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Represent ESCAP throughout North and Central Asia
  • Lead subregional development programmes
  • Support implementation of the Special Programme for Economies of Central Asia (SPECA)
  • Provide policy advice to senior UN leadership
  • Build partnerships with governments, development partners, civil society, and academia
  • Manage budgets, staffing, and programme delivery
  • Support regional sustainable development initiatives

Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Master’s degree in Economics, Social Sciences, or a related field
  • Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible professional experience
  • Significant international-level experience
  • Strong policy formulation and programme management expertise
  • Experience working with governments and international organizations

Why Apply?

This role offers a unique opportunity to influence development policy and regional cooperation across one of the world’s most strategically important regions.

Click here for more and to apply

2. Programme Officer (P-3)

Location

Bangkok, Thailand

Application Deadline

23 June 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is recruiting a Programme Officer to support the implementation of the Energy Transition for Green Growth and Prosperity Programme.

This multi-year initiative focuses on accelerating sustainable energy transitions, promoting green industrial development, and supporting economic transformation throughout Asia-Pacific countries.

The successful candidate will work within the Financing for Development Section of the Macroeconomic Policy and Financing for Development Division.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support programme implementation and evaluation
  • Coordinate technical assistance projects
  • Conduct policy research and analysis
  • Support high-level advisory groups and donor engagement
  • Organize workshops and stakeholder consultations
  • Prepare reports, studies, and policy papers
  • Contribute to financing and energy transition initiatives
  • Support regional cooperation programmes

Areas of Focus

The role will contribute to work related to:

  • Energy transition financing
  • Green growth
  • Sustainable development
  • Regional cooperation
  • Power grid interconnection
  • Social development
  • Artificial intelligence and economic transformation
  • Macroeconomic policy

Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, Energy, International Relations, Business Administration, Political Science, or related fields
  • Minimum five years of relevant professional experience
  • Experience in financing energy transition projects
  • Experience supporting international development initiatives
  • Strong research and analytical capabilities

Why Apply?

This is an excellent opportunity for mid-career professionals interested in sustainable finance, energy transition, and international development.

Click here for more and to apply

 

3. Director of Administration (D-1)

Location

Bangkok, Thailand

Application Deadline

04 July 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is seeking a highly experienced executive leader to serve as Director of Administration.

The position oversees all major administrative operations supporting ESCAP and various UN entities across the Asia-Pacific region.

This is one of the most senior administrative leadership positions within ESCAP.

Key Responsibilities

The Director will lead operations covering:

  • Human resources management
  • Finance and budgeting
  • Procurement
  • Information technology services
  • Conference services
  • Real estate management
  • Central support services
  • Travel and transport services
  • Host country relations

The role also includes supporting major UN reform initiatives, modernization programmes, operational efficiency projects, and organizational transformation efforts.

Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • Master’s degree in Public Administration, Business Administration, Finance, Human Resources, Management, or related fields
  • Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible administrative experience
  • Experience leading large multi-functional teams
  • Strong knowledge of UN systems and procedures
  • Expertise in organizational transformation and operational management

Why Apply?

This position offers an opportunity to shape administrative excellence and operational innovation across one of the UN’s largest regional commissions.

Click here for more and to apply

4. Expert in Transport Finance (Consultant)

Duty Station

Bangkok, Thailand

Work Location

Fiji

Application Deadline

08 June 2026

Contract Duration

22 June 2026 – 31 December 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is recruiting a national consultant to support the development of Fiji’s Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) 3.0 Costed Implementation Plan (CIP).

The consultancy forms part of ESCAP’s regional project aimed at strengthening member states’ capacity to implement low-carbon and climate-resilient transport systems.

The assignment will help transform Fiji’s climate commitments into actionable and financially viable transport investments.

Project Goals

The consultant will contribute to:

  • Low-carbon transport development
  • Climate-resilient transport planning
  • Sustainable transport financing
  • Climate mitigation strategies
  • Green transport investment pipelines
  • NDC implementation

Key Responsibilities

Sector Analysis

  • Analyze Fiji’s transport mitigation targets
  • Review transport decarbonization strategies
  • Assess policy priorities

Project Pipeline Development

  • Identify transport mitigation projects
  • Estimate CAPEX and OPEX requirements
  • Assess investment gaps
  • Prioritize climate investments

Policy and Technical Support

  • Draft transport chapters for the Costed Implementation Plan
  • Develop monitoring and reporting frameworks
  • Integrate climate and gender considerations

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Participate in consultations and workshops
  • Present technical findings
  • Support validation processes

Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Advanced degree in Transport, Civil Engineering, Environmental Science, Climate Change, Energy Studies, or related fields
  • At least seven years of experience in:
    • Sustainable transport
    • Climate change mitigation
    • Transport decarbonization
    • Transport finance
    • Transport policy analysis
  • Knowledge of Fiji’s climate policies and institutional framework
  • Strong expertise in transport modelling and GHG accounting

Why Apply?

This consultancy provides a unique opportunity to directly contribute to Fiji’s climate transition strategy and support sustainable transport investments with long-term environmental and economic impact.

Click here for more and to apply

 

Important Application Deadlines

Applicants should carefully note the following deadlines:

  1. Expert in Transport Finance – 08 June 2026
  2. Programme Officer (P-3) – 23 June 2026
  3. Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1) – 27 June 2026
  4. Director of Administration (D-1) – 04 July 2026

Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to submit applications well before the closing dates.

Why Consider a Career with ESCAP?

Working with ESCAP provides professionals with the opportunity to:

  • Influence regional and global development policy
  • Collaborate with governments and international organizations
  • Support climate action and sustainable development initiatives
  • Build experience within the United Nations system
  • Contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals
  • Work in diverse multicultural environments
  • Lead impactful projects affecting millions of people across Asia and the Pacific

For professionals seeking meaningful international careers, these ESCAP vacancies represent exceptional opportunities to create lasting impact while advancing their careers within the United Nations system.

 

For more global job opportunities click here

 

The post United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI. The programme is […]

The post Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI.

The programme is seeking 3–4 fellows for its current cohort and offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world’s leading centres for AI ethics research.

About the Accelerator Fellowship Programme

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative of the Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI. The programme was created in response to the growing ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and adoption of artificial intelligence technologies across society.

Unlike traditional research fellowships that focus primarily on academic outputs, this programme emphasizes real-world impact. It seeks to support projects that can shape the development of AI in ethical and socially beneficial directions while extending the influence of research already taking place within the Institute.

The fellowship is grounded in:

  • Philosophical inquiry
  • Academic independence
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Practical impact
  • Responsible innovation

Through this approach, Oxford aims to bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI ethics research and meaningful implementation in society.

Fellowship Objectives

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme supports work that:

  • Creates significant impact beyond academia
  • Promotes the responsible and ethical development of AI
  • Aligns with one or more research priorities of the Institute for Ethics in AI
  • Amplifies and extends the impact of existing research at the Institute

The programme encourages fellows to transform research insights into practical initiatives capable of influencing decision-making, industry practices, public understanding, and governance frameworks.

Types of Projects Supported

Oxford’s Accelerator Fellowship Programme welcomes a diverse range of initiatives focused on ethical AI. Potential areas of impact include, but are not limited to:

1. AI Policy and Governance Innovation

Projects that contribute to:

  • AI regulation
  • Governance frameworks
  • Public policy development
  • Ethical standards and accountability systems
  • International cooperation on AI governance

2. Professional Development and Capacity Building

Initiatives designed to:

  • Train AI professionals in ethical practices
  • Create educational opportunities
  • Develop responsible AI leadership
  • Build ethics competencies across sectors

3. Commercial and Technical Innovation

Projects that support:

  • Responsible AI products
  • Ethical AI tools and systems
  • Industry best practices
  • Commercial solutions that prioritize fairness, transparency, and accountability

4. Strategic Networks and Partnerships

Efforts focused on:

  • Building alliances across sectors
  • Connecting academia, industry, and government
  • Facilitating interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Creating sustainable AI ethics ecosystems

5. Public Engagement and Awareness

Projects aimed at:

  • Improving public understanding of AI
  • Encouraging informed discussions about AI ethics
  • Increasing public participation in AI-related decision-making
  • Transforming public discourse around emerging technologies

What Makes a Strong Application?

The Institute has clearly stated that it is looking for projects that are already on a well-defined path toward impact.

Applicants should note that this fellowship is not intended for:

  • Early-stage concepts
  • Idea-stage proposals
  • Proof-of-concept projects
  • Blue-sky research without implementation plans

Instead, successful applicants are expected to demonstrate:

Existing Project Development

Projects should already be underway, with evidence of meaningful progress.

Established Partnerships

Applicants should have:

  • Existing collaborators
  • Strategic partners already engaged
  • Clearly identified stakeholders who will support implementation

Defined Delivery Roadmap

Applications should include:

  • Concrete milestones
  • Realistic timelines
  • Deliverables
  • Implementation strategies

Impact Measurement Framework

Applicants must explain:

  • How impact will be achieved
  • How outcomes will be measured
  • What indicators will be used to evaluate success

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence is increasingly shaping economies, governments, healthcare systems, education, security, and everyday life.

As AI capabilities continue to expand, ethical considerations have become more important than ever. Questions around fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, safety, and societal impact require practical solutions that go beyond academic discussion.

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme seeks to bridge this gap by supporting leaders who can transform ethical AI research into actionable solutions that benefit society.

Fellowship Benefits

Although detailed funding information is provided within the fellowship’s expanded sections, participants can generally expect:

  • Affiliation with the University of Oxford’s Institute for Ethics in AI
  • Access to world-class expertise and networks
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Support in accelerating project impact
  • Increased visibility for ethical AI initiatives
  • Engagement with leading researchers, policymakers, and industry practitioners

Number of Fellowships Available

The Institute for Ethics in AI is seeking to recruit:

3–4 Accelerator Fellows

for the current cohort.

Given the limited number of positions available, applicants are encouraged to prepare strong, evidence-based proposals demonstrating both readiness and potential impact.

Application Process

Interested applicants should:

  1. Review the official eligibility criteria carefully.
  2. Ensure their project aligns with at least one Institute research priority.
  3. Demonstrate existing project development and partnerships.
  4. Prepare a clear impact strategy and implementation roadmap.
  5. Submit their application through the official Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI channels.

Apply Here 

Important Contact Information

For fellowship-related enquiries:

Email: aiethicsafp@philosophy.ox.ac.uk

For general enquiries:

Email: aiethicscomms@philosophy.ox.ac.uk

Final Thoughts

The Oxford Accelerator Fellowship Programme represents a unique opportunity for researchers, innovators, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and practitioners working at the intersection of artificial intelligence and ethics.

By supporting projects that are already positioned for impact, the programme aims to accelerate real-world solutions that promote responsible AI development, strengthen governance frameworks, build strategic collaborations, and improve public understanding of AI.

For individuals and teams committed to ensuring that artificial intelligence develops in ways that are ethical, inclusive, and socially beneficial, this fellowship offers access to one of the world’s most influential platforms for AI ethics leadership.

For more information on this fellowship visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

 

 

The post Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Scholars Incubator Program offered by the Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) is a prestigious international research development initiative designed to support, mentor, and empower emerging scholars in the fields of social sciences and MENA-focused research. This program is not just a short-term training course—it is a structured academic incubation ecosystem that provides junior […]

The post Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Scholars Incubator Program offered by the Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) is a prestigious international research development initiative designed to support, mentor, and empower emerging scholars in the fields of social sciences and MENA-focused research.

This program is not just a short-term training course—it is a structured academic incubation ecosystem that provides junior researchers, graduate students, and early-career scholars with the institutional support, mentorship, and research environment needed to advance their academic careers on a global scale.

Based in Europe and focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, SSL MENA is an independent, non-governmental, and non-partisan research institution that fosters interdisciplinary collaboration and academic excellence. The Scholars Incubator Program is one of its flagship initiatives, designed to bridge the gap between early academic potential and global research opportunities.

About Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA)

The Social Sciences Lab MENA is a European-based independent research institution dedicated to building a global scholarly community focused on MENA studies.

The Lab brings together researchers from different academic disciplines, ideologies, and regions to encourage collaboration, knowledge exchange, and high-quality research output.

Its core mission includes:

  • Developing interdisciplinary research networks focused on the MENA region
  • Supporting emerging scholars in academic publishing and funding applications
  • Providing institutional affiliation to researchers worldwide
  • Facilitating field research and research support services
  • Connecting scholars across senior and junior academic levels
  • Promoting independent research and intellectual freedom

Unlike traditional institutions, SSL MENA does not impose a fixed research agenda. Instead, it empowers scholars to pursue their own research interests with institutional guidance and support.

What is the Scholars Incubator Program?

The Scholars Incubator Program is a structured academic development fellowship designed to help emerging researchers strengthen their academic identity, research capabilities, and publication potential.

It provides participants with:

  • Academic mentorship and supervision
  • Research collaboration opportunities
  • Institutional affiliation with a European research institute
  • Publishing and funding application support
  • Access to academic networks and research communities

The program is designed to be both intellectually rigorous and professionally supportive, ensuring scholars develop practical research skills while advancing their academic careers.

Program Vision and Philosophy

The Scholars Incubator Program is built on the belief that researchers should have intellectual freedom, structured support, and collaborative opportunities.

Key principles include:

1. Researcher-Centered Approach

Participants are encouraged to develop independent research ideas rather than follow a fixed institutional agenda.

2. Inclusive Academic Environment

The program welcomes scholars from diverse backgrounds, ideologies, and academic traditions, provided they adhere to research ethics and academic standards.

3. Interdisciplinary Collaboration

Scholars from different disciplines work together to enrich research perspectives and outcomes.

4. Long-Term Academic Growth

The program is designed not just for short-term training but for long-term academic career development.

Key Benefits of the Scholars Incubator Program

Participants in the program gain access to a wide range of academic, professional, and institutional benefits.

1. Research Affiliation

  • One-year affiliation with the Social Sciences Lab MENA
  • Possible extension through continued engagement with the Lab Community
  • Recognition of academic participation and training

2. Mentorship and Academic Supervision

  • Personalized mentorship from experienced scholars
  • Guidance on research design and methodology
  • Support in developing academic career pathways

3. Publishing and Academic Writing Support

Participants receive:

  • Guidance on academic publishing processes
  • Editing and proofreading support
  • Assistance in preparing journal submissions
  • Feedback on research manuscripts

4. Funding and Career Development Support

The program provides structured guidance for:

  • Applying to research grants and fellowships
  • European funding schemes such as:
    • ERC (European Research Council)
    • Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions
    • Erasmus+ programs
  • Research position applications across academic institutions

5. Access to Research Resources

Scholars benefit from:

  • Access to research data and institutional resources
  • Participation in collaborative research projects
  • Fieldwork and methodological support
  • Integration into thematic research groups

6. Networking and Academic Community

Participants join a global scholarly network that includes:

  • Junior and senior researchers
  • Principal investigators
  • Guest scholars and experts
  • Interdisciplinary academic communities

This network enables long-term academic collaboration and professional development.

7. Certification and Institutional Recognition

Participants receive:

  • Affiliation certificate from SSL MENA
  • Recognition of training participation
  • Support letters when required for academic applications

Program Structure and Learning Experience

The Scholars Incubator Program is structured to provide a balanced academic and practical experience.

Core Activities Include:

1. Mentorship Sessions

  • 30 minutes monthly (individual sessions)
  • Focused academic guidance and progress review

2. Monthly Group Sessions

  • 1-hour collaborative academic discussions
  • Peer learning and research exchange

3. Workshops and Opportunities Sessions

  • 1-hour monthly training sessions
  • Focus on research tools, publishing, and funding applications

4. Focus Zone Sessions
